sourceMovie.title: Max
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Max works as a adventure movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The adventure and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Call of the Wild
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Max clicked for you, The Call of the Wild (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: A Dog's Purpose
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Max clicked for you, A Dog's Purpose (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Max?
faq.answer: Try The Call of the Wild, A Dog's Purpose, Eight Below—each shares DNA with Max while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Max?
faq.answer: Max (2015) is best described as Adventure, Drama, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Max part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Max Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
